A brute force O(n²) approach checks all subarrays. Note: because of negative numbers, a sliding window won't work here.
The key insight: `sum(i..j) = prefixSum[j] - prefixSum[i-1]`. So if `prefixSum[j] - k` was seen before, there's a valid subarray.
Use a HashMap to count occurrences of each prefix sum. Initialize with `{0: 1}` to handle subarrays starting from index 0.
For each element, compute running prefix sum. Check if `prefixSum - k` is in the map. Add the count, then record the current prefix sum in the map.
